  • DashboardQuickActionsTests - Verify that the Refresh Dashboard button is disabled during an active refresh to prevent duplicate API requests

    3:39:02 pm / 00:00:02:579 Fail

    DashboardQuickActionsTests - Verify that the Refresh Dashboard button is disabled during an active refresh to prevent duplicate API requests
    04.03.2026 3:39:02 pm 04.03.2026 3:39:04 pm 00:00:02:579 · #test-id=45
    QAAutomation DASHBOARD_QUICK_ACTIONS
    • Pass
    • 30:00:000
    Test Case Details
    StatusTimestampDetails
    Info 3:39:02 pm
    Test DescriptionExpected Outcome
    Verify that the Refresh Dashboard button is disabled during an active refresh to prevent duplicate API requestsThe button has the 'disabled' HTML attribute immediately after clicking. After the refresh cycle completes the button transitions back to enabled.
    • Pass
    • 30:00:000
    Test Case Meta Data
    StatusTimestampDetails
    Info 3:39:02 pm
    ReferencesBugs
    [PS-6][]
    • Fail
    • 30:02:578
    Test Case Execution Steps
    StatusTimestampDetails
    Info 3:39:02 pm Step [Test Step : #1] - Re-navigate to the Dashboard and confirm Refresh button is enabled before any action
    Info 3:39:02 pm Step [Test Step : #2] - Verify the Refresh button is not disabled before initiating a refresh
    Info 3:39:02 pm Step [Test Step : #3] - Click the Refresh Dashboard button to initiate a refresh cycle
    Info 3:39:04 pm Step [Test Step : #4] - Immediately assert the Refresh button now has the 'disabled' attribute — prevents duplicate requests
    Info 3:39:04 pm Step [Test Step : #5] - Wait for the refresh cycle to complete — KPI cards repopulate
    Pass 3:39:04 pm Verify css=div.bg-card:has(div[class*='text-xs']:text-is('Total Devices')) div[class*='text-3xl'] is visible on UI
    Info 3:39:04 pm Step [Test Step : #6] - Verify the Refresh button transitions back to an enabled state after the refresh completes
    Fail 3:39:04 pm
    Screenshot
    Fail 3:39:04 pm Test finished with result: Fail
    Fail 3:39:04 pm Exception : Refresh button should be re-enabled after the refresh cycle completes expected [false] but found [true]

    Status Timestamp TestName
    Pass 15:38:15 pm DashboardQuickActionsTests - Verify that Quick Action card badge counts are re-rendered with valid numeric data after the Refresh Dashboard button completes a refresh cycle

    Pass 15:39:12 pm DashboardQuickActionsTests - Verify that the 'Schedule Service' quick action card shows an orange badge with a positive integer count of scheduled service orders when orders are scheduled
    Fail 15:39:15 pm DashboardQuickActionsTests - Verify that clicking the 'Schedule Service' quick action card navigates to the /account-service route
    Pass 15:39:27 pm DashboardQuickActionsTests - Verify that sidebar navigation badge selectors are isolated from Quick Action card badge selectors — they do not overlap and they show consistent counts for the same routes
    Pass 15:39:30 pm DashboardQuickActionsTests - Verify that the 'View Inventory' quick action card shows an orange badge with a positive integer count of offline devices when devices are offline
    Fail 15:39:32 pm DashboardQuickActionsTests - Verify that clicking the 'View Inventory' quick action card navigates to the /inventory route

    Pass 15:39:47 pm DashboardQuickActionsTests - Verify that the Dashboard welcome message displays the logged-in user's email address after login
